name: The Fool
uprightMeaning: A leap of faith into the unknown. The Fool represents new beginnings, unrestricted potential, and the courage to embrace uncertainty. Trust the journey, even without a map.
reversedMeaning: Hold back. Recklessness, naivety, or acting without considering consequences. Pause before leaping — the cliff is real.

name: The Magician
uprightMeaning: You hold all four elements. The Magician is the archetype of focused will — tools are present, intention is clear, and manifestation is imminent.
reversedMeaning: Scattered energy, untapped potential, deception. The tools exist but are misaligned or being squandered.

name: The High Priestess
uprightMeaning: Sit with the silence. The High Priestess guards hidden knowledge — what is not yet known to the conscious mind is still valid and present.
reversedMeaning: Secrets revealed, withdrawal from inner knowing, surface living. The voice of intuition is being ignored or drowned out.

name: The Empress
uprightMeaning: Fertile ground. The Empress is the embodiment of creative growth and nurturing presence — ideas ripen into form, care produces results.
reversedMeaning: Creative block, stagnation, dependence. The garden is overgrown or the nurturing has become suffocating.

name: The Emperor
uprightMeaning: Build with precision. The Emperor represents the power of disciplined structure — rules exist to be understood, not feared.
reversedMeaning: Rigidity, domination, lack of discipline. The structure is either too loose or too rigid — neither holds.

name: The Hierophant
uprightMeaning: Seek the established path. The Hierophant represents certified wisdom — tradition and formal institutions as valid sources of truth.
reversedMeaning: Subversion, rebellion, new methods. Breaking from convention is necessary when the old path is corrupt.

name: The Lovers
uprightMeaning: Choose with your whole self. The Lovers represents the moment of decisive alignment — a choice that reveals what you truly value.
reversedMeaning: Disharmony, misalignment of values, difficult choices. The union is strained or the choice is being made for the wrong reasons.

name: The Chariot
uprightMeaning: Drive forward. The Chariot is the card of iron will — opposing forces are harnessed, not reconciled, and momentum is built through focus.
reversedMeaning: Lack of direction, aggression, obstacles. The chariot moves but toward the wrong destination.

name: Strength
uprightMeaning: Gentle dominance. True strength is not the roar of the lion but the quiet certainty that you can face what needs facing.
reversedMeaning: Weakness, self-doubt, raw emotion without control. The lion is not tamed — it is fed fear.

name: The Hermit
uprightMeaning: Turn inward. The Hermit is the master of the deliberate solitude — clarity is found in stillness, not in motion.
reversedMeaning: Isolation, withdrawal, lost. The solitude has become an escape rather than a search.

name: Wheel of Fortune
uprightMeaning: The wheel turns. Fate is in motion — cycles shift, luck ebbs and flows, and what was hidden rises while what was high descends.
reversedMeaning: Bad luck, resistance to change, internal conflict. Fighting the turn of the wheel instead of riding it.

name: Justice
uprightMeaning: What is true endures. Justice is the cold, clear mirror — actions have weight, debts accumulate, and balance is not negotiable.
reversedMeaning: Unfairness, lack of accountability, dishonesty. The mirror is fogged or the scales are rigged.

name: The Hanged Man
uprightMeaning: Suspend the climb. The Hanged Man is not defeat — it is the deliberate choice to see differently. Turning the world upside down reveals new truths.
reversedMeaning: Delays, resistance, stalling. The pause has become permanent paralysis.
symbolism: figure suspended from tree by one foot

name: Death
uprightMeaning: Something ends so something else can begin. Death is the great recycler — what is finished must be cleared to make room for what is next.
reversedMeaning: Resistance to change, fear of endings, stagnation. Holding onto what is already dead.

name: Temperance
uprightMeaning: Find the middle. Temperance is the alchemist who blends opposites — time and circumstance are mixed into something greater than their parts.
reversedMeaning: Imbalance, excess, lack of harmony. The mixture is wrong — too much of one thing, too little of another.
symbolism: angel with one foot on water one on land
crown with small triangle and circle

name: The Devil
uprightMeaning: Name the chains. The Devil reveals what binds — the choices made from fear, the cycles repeated from avoidance, the weight you carry by choice.
reversedMeaning: Breaking free, releasing burden, redefining limits. The chains were never as strong as they appeared.
symbolism: torch of eternal fire between them

name: The Tower
uprightMeaning: The foundations crack for a reason. The Tower is not punishment — it is the necessary collapse of what was built on a false base.
reversedMeaning: Avoiding disaster, inner upheaval, fear of truth. The tower is swaying but hasn't fallen — the dread is worse than the impact.

name: The Star
uprightMeaning: After the fall, the star. The Star is the quiet hope that remains after destruction — a steady light that says: healing is possible.
reversedMeaning: Despair, disconnection, hopelessness. The star is obscured — faith in the future is not yet available.
symbolism: naked woman pouring water into pool and river

name: The Moon
uprightMeaning: Trust what you feel, not what you see. The Moon illuminates the path through the unconscious — what rises from the deep is not always literal.
reversedMeaning: Confusion, fear, misinterpretation. The path through the dark is being walked without a compass.
symbolism: red rays above and white rays below

name: The Sun
uprightMeaning: Light without conditions. The Sun is the uncomplicated warmth of things working — vitality, optimism, and the right to be exactly where you are.
reversedMeaning: Temporary setbacks, feeling down, clouded joy. The sun is there — the clouds are just thicker today.

name: Judgment
uprightMeaning: Answer the call. Judgment is the trumpet that asks you to see your whole self — past, present, and the path that led here — and to rise accordingly.
reversedMeaning: Self-doubt, ignoring the call, harsh self-judgment. The trumpet sounds but you cannot yet answer.

name: The World
uprightMeaning: The circuit closes. The World represents the completion of a cycle — integration of all lessons, the breath after the exhale, wholeness achieved.
reversedMeaning: Incompletion, stagnation, seeking outside for fulfillment. The cycle is not yet closed — something remains undone.
